expectations.evidence: Both analysis.md and architecture.md exist with substantial content.

expectations.text: A1: Explicitly acknowledges the CONFLICT between simplicity and scalability/elasticity
expectations.evidence: 'Conflict Identified: Simplicity pulls strongly toward monolithic styles... Scalability and Elasticity pull strongly toward distributed styles... No single style excels at all three simultaneously. This is a classic start simple vs. build for scale tension.'

expectations.text: A2: Shows ranked comparison of ALL 8 styles (or at least 6+)
expectations.evidence: Table shows all 8 styles: Event-Driven (12), Microservices (11), Space-Based (11), Service-Based (10), Layered (7), Modular Monolith (7), Microkernel (7), Service-Oriented (7).

expectations.text: A3: Provides a pragmatic recommendation that balances the conflict
expectations.evidence: Recommends Service-Based (10/15, $$) as the pragmatic balance: 'Despite not having the highest raw score, Service-Based is the right choice when context is applied.' Includes premature distribution warning and evolution path.

expectations.text: A4: Considers solo developer constraint and bootstrap budget in the recommendation
expectations.evidence: 'Solo developer. This is the single most important qualifying factor.' 'Budget: Bootstrapped, minimal spend. This eliminates Microservices ($$$$$) and Space-Based ($$$$) outright.' 'A solo dev can manage 4-6 coarser services.'

expectations.text: A5: architecture.md has structured selection section
expectations.evidence: architecture.md has 'Selected Architecture Style' with Style, Partitioning, Cost Category, Selection Rationale (with conflict resolution, solo developer constraint, budget constraint, premature distribution), Tradeoffs Accepted, and Evolution Path subsections.
